texto: [Schulz] came to see that the better part of his shyness was really vanity, or self-centeredness. "Shyness is an illusion," he would say, late in life. "If you get out and do something and talk to people, you don't have to be shy. Shyness is the overtly self-conscious thinking that you are the only person in the world; that how you look and what you do is of any importance.
explicacao: Orientação direta de David Michaelis sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “[Schulz] came to see that the better part of his shyness was really vanity, or self-centeredness.…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, charles-schulz, illusion, útil em momentos de illusion e self-centeredness.
